result.question: What techniques, tools, and recent developments (up to 2026-07-16) can speed up the colibri engine running GLM-5.2 744B MoE (int4, NVMe expert streaming, 32 GB RAM / RTX 5060 8 GB, Windows 11) without losing output quality — given that per-forward expert loading from disk is the bottleneck, native MTP speculation already yields 2.0 tok/forward, and GPU-draft IPC plus output compression were already tried and rejected?
result.summary: The bottleneck is verification-side disk I/O, not drafting, so the queries split the space into: (1) upstream colibri features/benchmarks we haven't enabled, (2) lossless speculative-decoding advances that could stack with MTP, (3) idea-mining from competing MoE-offload engines and expert-prefetch research, (4) Windows-specific NVMe/I-O engineering for weight streaming, and (5) practitioner-measured recipes plus first-half-2026 novelties from the local-LLM community.

angles.label: Upstream colibri features & maintainer benchmarks
angles.query: github JustVugg colibri engine CACHE_ROUTE PILOT PIPE expert streaming speedup discussion 199 207 benchmark GLM
angles.rationale: Surfaces the repo's own commits, PRs, issues and discussions (#199 CACHE_ROUTE, #207) plus forks, revealing turn-on-now env knobs and maintainer-measured gains directly applicable to our build.

angles.label: Lossless speculative decoding advances 2025-2026
angles.query: EAGLE-3 lossless speculative decoding 2026 multi-token prediction MTP tree draft adaptive depth verification-bound MoE acceptance rate
angles.rationale: Finds quality-neutral speculation methods (EAGLE-2/3, dynamic depth, tree/self-speculation, multi-draft) and evidence on which help when the verifier — not the drafter — is the bottleneck, as in our disk-streamed 744B forward.

angles.label: Competing MoE-offload engines & expert prefetch research
angles.query: KTransformers PowerInfer-2 MoE-Infinity llama.cpp n-cpu-moe expert prefetch prediction LRU cache NVMe offload inference 2025 2026
angles.rationale: Steals proven techniques from adjacent engines and academic expert-locality/prefetching work — routing-aware caching, layer-ahead prefetch, hot-expert pinning — that map onto colibri's per-layer LRU + streaming architecture.

angles.label: Windows NVMe I/O engineering for weight streaming
angles.query: Windows DirectStorage CPU unbuffered overlapped IO IORING NVMe queue depth mmap large model weight streaming performance
angles.rationale: Targets the actual bottleneck: raw disk-to-RAM throughput on Windows 11 — DirectStorage for non-GPU payloads, FILE_FLAG_NO_BUFFERING/overlapped reads, queue-depth tuning, mmap vs read tradeoffs, and RAM-disk hybrids for hot experts within 32 GB.

angles.label: Community-measured recipes & H1-2026 novelties
angles.query: r/LocalLLaMA GLM-5.2 GLM-4.5 MoE local NVMe SSD streaming tok/s colibri Hacker News 2026 quantization config speedup
angles.rationale: Captures practitioner benchmarks and config recipes from Reddit/HN (colibri's July 2026 HN thread), plus brand-new 2026 releases — engines, GLM variants with better MTP heads, quant formats with negligible quality loss — that formal sources may not cover yet.

results.title: MoE-Infinity: Efficient MoE Inference on Personal Machines with Sparsity-Aware Expert Cache
results.snippet: The closest academic analog to colibri's architecture: streams experts from slow storage on a personal machine and shows that a plain LRU expert cache is suboptimal. Introduces an Expert Activation Matrix that tracks per-sequence expert reuse to drive activation-aware caching and prefetching — a drop-in idea for upgrading colibri's per-layer LRU without any quality impact (caching is lossless).

results.title: PreScope: Unleashing the Power of Prefetching for Resource-Constrained MoE Inference
results.snippet: Directly targets the exact bottleneck colibri has (expert loading dominates, not compute/drafting). Uses a learned cross-layer expert-activation predictor plus prefetch scheduling that overlaps expert I/O with computation of earlier layers; reports 141% throughput gain and 74.6% decode-latency reduction over SOTA offloading baselines. Maps onto strengthening colibri's PILOT/PIPE prefetch path. Lossless — only changes when/what is loaded, not the math.

results.title: DALI: A Workload-Aware Offloading Framework for Efficient MoE Inference on Local PCs
results.snippet: Brand-new 2026 work explicitly scoped to local consumer PCs. Its Residual-Based Prefetching exploits the similarity of hidden states between adjacent MoE layers to predict next-layer expert routing early — i.e., run layer L's router input against layer L+1's gate ahead of time to start NVMe reads. This is the concrete 'layer-ahead prefetch' mechanism the research angle asks for, implementable in colibri's per-layer streaming loop with zero quality risk.

results.title: KTransformers: Unleashing the Full Potential of CPU/GPU Hybrid Inference for MoE Models (SOSP 2025)
results.snippet: The peer-reviewed systems paper behind the leading practical engine for running DeepSeek/GLM-class MoE on desktops. Details expert placement policy (hot experts on GPU, cold on CPU), optimized sparse CPU kernels, and CPU-GPU expert scheduling (Jan 2026 release adds CPU-GPU Expert Scheduling). Best single source of proven, engineering-grade ideas to port into colibri, e.g. pinning the empirically hottest experts in the 8 GB VRAM / RAM budget — an extension of the CACHE_ROUTE direction.

results.title: HybriMoE: Hybrid CPU-GPU Scheduling and Cache Management for Efficient MoE Inference
results.snippet: Built on the kTransformers framework; contributes an impact-driven prefetching algorithm and a priority-queue cache that keeps 'hot' experts resident — a direct alternative to colibri's per-layer LRU eviction policy. Its simulation-based scheduling of which experts to keep vs. stream is applicable even when the backing store is NVMe rather than CPU RAM. Quality-neutral (pure systems change).

results.title: Guide to optimizing inference performance of large MoE models across CPU+GPU using llama.cpp and its derivatives
results.snippet: Practical community playbook for llama.cpp / ik_llama.cpp MoE offload: --n-cpu-moe, --override-tensor regex placement of ffn_*_exps tensors, and which layers to keep on GPU. Useful for measured, real-world config recipes and for understanding what the competing engine ecosystem achieves on similar consumer hardware, though it assumes experts fit in RAM rather than colibri's disk-streaming regime.

results.title: MoE-SpeQ: Speculative Quantized Decoding with Proactive Expert Prefetching and Offloading for Mixture-of-Experts
results.snippet: Closest match to colibri's exact bottleneck: uses the draft model's speculated tokens to PREDICT which experts the future tokens will route to and prefetches them from host/offload storage ahead of verification, overlapping expert I/O with compute. Includes an adaptive governor (Amortization Roofline Model) that tunes speculation depth to the hardware's I/O cost — a direct blueprint for turning colibri's MTP drafts into expert-prefetch hints for the NVMe-streamed 744B verifier, losslessly.

results.title: Accelerating Mixture-of-Experts Inference by Hiding Offloading Latency with Speculative Decoding
results.snippet: Directly frames speculative decoding not as a compute saver but as a way to hide expert-offloading (disk/PCIe) latency in MoE inference — the exact regime where colibri's ~13 s forward is dominated by expert loading across 78 layers. Evidence that spec decoding pays off in offloaded MoE precisely because verifying k drafted tokens amortizes one expensive expert-fetch pass over multiple tokens.

results.title: Making Every Verified Token Count: Adaptive Verification for MoE Speculative Decoding
results.snippet: 2026 paper on the verification side of MoE speculative decoding: shows MoE-specific verification cost dominates end-to-end efficiency and proposes adaptive verification scheduling to maximize accepted tokens per (expensive) verification pass. Directly addresses the 'which speculation techniques help when the verifier is the bottleneck' question; candidate for tuning colibri's draft-length/acceptance policy around its 60% first-token acceptance.

results.title: Self-Speculative Decoding for On-device MoE Acceleration (WWW 2026)
results.snippet: WWW 2026 work on self-speculation for MoE models running on resource-constrained devices — no external draft model needed (avoids the GPU-draft IPC approach colibri already tried and rejected). Uses the MoE model itself (e.g., reduced-expert or dense-part-only forward) as the drafter, which maps naturally onto colibri's RAM-resident ~10 GB dense component drafting for the disk-streamed full model.

results.title: EntMTP: Accelerating LLM Inference with Entropy-Guided Multi-Token Prediction
results.snippet: June 2026: entropy-guided adaptive depth for native MTP heads — draft more tokens when the MTP head is confident, stop early when entropy is high, cutting wasted verification work. Stacks directly on top of colibri's existing GLM-5.2 native MTP (60% acceptance) without a new draft model, and is quality-neutral since all tokens are still verified.

results.title: MoE-Spec: Expert Budgeting for Efficient Speculative Decoding
results.snippet: Feb 2026: instead of backing off speculation when MoE verification is expensive, it reduces verification cost itself by budgeting which experts get activated during the verify pass. Highly relevant to colibri's expert-loading bottleneck, but flag for scrutiny: expert budgeting can deviate from the exact output distribution, so quality-neutrality must be checked against the 'lossless' requirement (unlike EAGLE-3-style exact verification, which per vLLM/AMD reports sustains 70-80% acceptance but needs a trained draft head).

results.title: JustVugg/colibri — main repo (README with all env knobs and maintainer-measured features)
results.snippet: Canonical source for turn-on-now features: PILOT=1 router-lookahead prefetch (layer L+1's router applied to layer L's state recalls 71.6% of true top-8 experts vs 41.3% for last-token reuse, with a dedicated I/O prefetch thread), KVSAVE warm-restart of compressed MLA KV (~182 KB/token, zero re-prefill), DIRECT/O_DIRECT I/O, and — since 2026-07-10 — the expert LRU cache auto-sizes UP to fill the --ram budget and the learning cache auto-pins the hottest experts from your real usage at startup. Several of these are newer than the knobs the team already knows and directly address the disk-streaming bottleneck.

results.title: colibri maintainer experiment doc: GLM-5.2 on 6x5090 (2026-07-12) — verification-bottleneck measurements
results.snippet: Maintainer-measured data explaining exactly why deeper speculation won't help this setup: per-forward expert time scales almost linearly with verify-batch size S (80 ms at S=1, 168 ms at S=2, 306 ms at S=4) because the S positions route to mostly different experts — the failure is structural, not tuning. Also documents MTP-head quantization sensitivity (int4 MTP: acceptance collapses to 0–4%; int8 MTP: 39–59% acceptance, 2.2–2.8 tok/forward) and full-resident reference numbers (6.28–6.84 tok/s). Directly bounds which speculative-decoding advances are even applicable.

results.title: colibri Discussions #209: "Open problems — the measured frontier (pick one)"
results.snippet: The maintainer's curated list of unresolved, measurement-validated optimization frontiers (posted Jul 14, 2026). This is the roadmap of what upstream considers the highest-value speed work still open — the best place to see which of our candidate optimizations upstream has already measured, rejected, or flagged as promising, and to align our contribution with maintainer benchmarks.

results.title: colibri Discussions #208: "Post your benchmark here — datapoints, A/Bs, cold→warm curves"
results.snippet: Community benchmark thread started by the maintainer (Jul 14, 2026) collecting A/B datapoints and cold→warm curves across hardware. Useful for comparing our 32 GB / RTX 5060 / NVMe numbers against similar setups and for finding config recipes (DIRECT/PILOT/CACHE_ROUTE combinations) that others measured as wins.

results.title: colibri Discussions #291: "Pulling the model from dual SSDs at once — small speedup"
results.snippet: Community-measured expert-streaming optimization: striping model reads across two SSDs in parallel yields a small but real speedup. Directly relevant to our disk-bound bottleneck (13 s/forward dominated by expert loading) as a cheap hardware/layout change; related idea threads #272 (full tiered expert streaming) and #282 (master expert cache for new users) live in the same Ideas section.

results.title: HuggingFace: GLM-5.2-colibri-int4-with-int8-mtp (int4 body + int8 MTP head conversion)
results.snippet: Ready-made colibri-format conversion that keeps the model int4 but the MTP head at int8 — the exact quantization split the maintainer's experiments show is required for MTP speculation to work (int4 head: 0–4% acceptance; int8 head: 39–59%). Worth checking our own ~370 GB conversion uses this recipe; if our MTP head is int4 or fp-degraded, swapping the head alone is a zero-quality-risk acceptance-rate fix.

results.title: llama.cpp Discussion #18758: Mmap faster than direct I/O for MoE models
results.snippet: Discutie direct pe tradeoff-ul central pentru colibri: mmap vs direct/unbuffered I/O pentru expert streaming la MoE. Concluzia comunitatii: mmap castiga la multi-turn (page cache pastreaza expertii hot intre forward-uri, exact scenariul warm TTFT), dar page-fault handler-ul adauga jitter la accese reci non-secventiale — argument pentru un hibrid: mmap pentru hot set + unbuffered overlapped reads pentru cold experts. Aplicabil imediat la knob-urile DIRECT/PILOT ale engine-ului.

results.title: Tom's Hardware: Windows 11 native NVMe driver — up to 85% higher random workload performance
results.snippet: Windows 11 25H2 include noul driver NVMe nativ (nvmedisk.sys, construit pe IoRing), opt-in prin registry: +64–85% la 4K random read si pana la -47% cicluri CPU per I/O in benchmark-uri. Random reads mici pe NVMe sunt exact bottleneck-ul colibri (experti rutati per token pe 78 de layere), deci e o optimizare 'turn-on-now' la nivel de OS, zero risc de calitate. Atentie: Microsoft a blocat ulterior trick-ul initial de registry, dar exista workaround-uri documentate.

results.title: GitHub yardenshafir/IoRing_Demos: Windows I/O Ring demos, use cases and performance testing
results.snippet: Singura resursa practica cu cod pentru IoRing, echivalentul io_uring pe Windows 11 (BuildIoRingReadFile etc.): batch-uiesti sute de read-uri de experti intr-un singur submit cu completari asincrone, in loc de ReadFile/OVERLAPPED per expert. Cale de implementare concreta in C pur pentru a satura queue depth-ul NVMe-ului pe cele 78 de layere — categoria (b) 'implementable code change' pentru pipeline-ul PILOT/PIPE.

results.title: GitHub microsoft/DirectStorage: DirectStorage for Windows API + samples
results.snippet: Repo oficial cu API si sample-uri: DirectStorage suporta destinatie in system memory (nu doar VRAM), deschide fisierele unbuffered si e proiectat pentru ~50K IOPS la 5–10% dintr-un core CPU cu cozi lock-free — deci utilizabil pentru payload-uri CPU-only ca expertii colibri. Relevanta medie pentru ca avantajul fata de IoRing/overlapped bine scrise e incert pentru read-uri catre RAM (criticile gen 'DirectStorage is a poor PC port' sugereaza castig mic fara GPU decompression).

results.title: llama.cpp Issue #20757: Two-tier GPU+RAM expert cache for MoE offload (pluggable eviction policy)
results.snippet: Design in curs in llama.cpp pentru exact problema colibri: cache de experti pe doua niveluri (VRAM + RAM pinned) cu politica de evictie pluggable; pinned memory elimina copia extra la miss. Idei de furat pentru LRU-ul per-layer al colibri si pentru cei 8 GB de VRAM ai RTX 5060 folositi ca tier suplimentar de cache de experti hot, complementar cu CACHE_ROUTE.

results.title: HuggingFace: Over 2 tok/s NVMe-backed DeepSeek-R1 on 96GB RAM + 24GB VRAM with llama.cpp
results.snippet: Retetar practic din comunitate pentru MoE de sute de miliarde rulat cu experti streamati de pe NVMe via mmap: bandwidth masurat 2–5 GB/s la read in timpul inferentei, fara scrieri (nu e swap). Ordin de marime util ca baseline: daca colibri obtine mult sub asta pe NVMe-ul laptopului, problema e in I/O path (queue depth / buffered reads), nu in disc — justifica investitia in IoRing/unbuffered inainte de orice alta optimizare.
